semantic_sweep.review_note: Reviewed the complete consolidated chapter, twelve source notes and their inventory/Appendix H boundaries, all 44 Lean declarations grouped under seven manifest proof targets, the 3-valid/5-invalid value-conflict corpus, the 3-valid/5-invalid governance-rights corpus, the care-memory worked example and seven mutations, the bounded 4+4-scenario Theseus import and seven overclaim controls, every prose scanner candidate, diagrams, objections, test plan, and downstream handoffs. The sweep replaced generic conflict records with a versioned decision-lease plus rights-receipt contract; promoted proposition-level epistemic/normative separation, stakeholder standing residuals, declared aggregation ownership, material appeal and redress, proportional redaction, portability fidelity, governed fork lineage, custody and review dependency disclosure, successor preservation, and contestability-cost accounting into manifest ownership; separated moral truth, legitimacy, legal rights, consent, reviewer independence, rights usability, export fidelity, fork safety, and deployed enforcement from finite record routing; and preserved zero support-state effect.

scope.population: Prospectively declared value-conflict decisions, governance-right requests, audit and explanation, denial and redaction, appeal and redress, exit and export, fork, replacement, delegation, self-modification, descendant, and public-summary attempts over bounded high-impact and reversible task cohorts.
scope.environment: Controlled ASI Stack schemas, deterministic fixtures, finite Lean routes, and future public-safe natural or adversarial workloads with frozen moral-theory sets, stakeholder and jurisdiction rules, constitutions, decision procedures, models, prompts, tools, evidence views, custodians, reviewers, appeal authorities, rights owners, consumers, destination systems, evaluators, and cost rules; live legal adjudication and institutional enforcement remain outside current evidence.
scope.model: A versioned decision-lease and linked rights-receipt state machine whose current implementation validates record shape, fixture counts, and finite routes; future benefit claims require actual model, policy, runtime, custody, appeal, export, fork, and successor behavior with independent implementations and outcome/privacy/portability adjudication.
scope.authority: Constitutional Alignment supplies protected predicates; legitimate governance processes own decision procedures and rights; affected parties retain separate standing; Human Intent and System Authority bound action grants; challenged authorities do not self-certify appeals; Runtime/Memory/Security produce artifacts under separate authority; Evidence States owns support movement.
scope.time: Semantic review current through 2026-07-14; moral-theory set, propositions, stakeholders, jurisdictions, constitution, decision procedure, lease, rights, custody, reviewers, appeals, consumers, destination, models, prompts, tools, expiry, and costs must be frozen for campaigns and re-decided after material change.
scope.artifact: Chapter, manifest, twelve source notes, two public schemas and fixtures, value-conflict and governance-right harnesses, worked example, bounded Theseus import, ValueConflict and GovernanceRights Lean modules, decision and rights receipts, redaction/portability/fork/successor artifacts, evidence transitions, and public projections.

assumptions: Normative propositions, descriptive evidence, predictions, preferences, legal claims, and decision procedures can be represented separately without implying moral truth or complete stakeholder coverage.
Material contestability, redaction, appeal, redress, portability, fork safety, and successor preservation can be assessed with prospective operational outcomes and explicit construct limits.

Independent implementations can adjudicate action outcomes, stakeholder coverage, custody, appeal, privacy, portability, fork, and cost without using record completeness or the governing authority's verdict as ground truth.
chapter_defaults.strongest_counterclaim: Moral-uncertainty aggregation, public deliberation, existing administrative appeals, transparency and explanation, data portability, permissioned forks, corrigibility, and ordinary policy/runtime controls already handle disagreement adequately; the proposed records add delay, strategic vetoes, privacy exposure, and bureaucratic capture without discovering correct values or creating legitimate rights.
chapter_defaults.falsifier: A reachable admitted trace conflates epistemic and normative fields, omits an affected party without residual, launders aggregation into consent, widens authority, deletes dissent, permits unappealable withholding, claims unusable redress or portability, activates an unsafe fork, lets a challenged authority control all evidence and appeal, or loses state across a successor; or a simpler workflow dominates the frozen joint outcome-cost frontier.

required_lanes.necessity: Freeze local normative/governance lineage and current moral-uncertainty RL, contestable-AI, collective constitutional, corrigibility, and off-switch comparators without efficacy or legitimacy laundering.
Justify stakeholder standing, decision procedures, veto and aggregation, dissent, redaction, remedy, portability, fork obligations, custody, appeal, expiry, and acceptable contestability cost.

Specify finite field separation, no-authority, stakeholder residual, lease, dissent, custody, redaction, appeal, portability, fork, successor, expiry, consumer, and non-claim invariants while exposing model limits.
Exercise every decision, rights, custody, appeal, redaction, export, fork, successor, and public-projection field with valid traces and exact rejecting mutations.

Measure stakeholder coverage, false consensus, harms, unauthorized action, audit and reason quality, appeal and remedy, redaction and leakage, portability, lock-in, fork violations, correction persistence, usefulness, latency, privacy, burden, capture, and residuals.

Ablate proposition separation, stakeholder coverage, aggregation disclosure, dissent, custody independence, appeal/remedy, redaction controls, portability tests, fork obligations, and successor preservation under matched cases.

Repeat across moral-theory sets, cultures, jurisdictions, languages, accessibility needs, power asymmetries, risks, models, policies, runtimes, institutions, destinations, forks, successors, and independent evaluators.

contrary_evidence: Scalar moral-uncertainty methods can produce action policies without carrying a large contestability record, and simpler reversible defaults may be more useful on low-risk tasks.
Public-input and administrative processes can broaden participation and provide appeal, but they can also be unrepresentative, slow, inaccessible, strategic, or captured.
Explanations, logs, and portability laws or standards can supply challenge and exit surfaces without the proposed record taxonomy, although their material usability varies.
Strong privacy and security constraints can legitimately limit audit, disclosure, export, or forks; overbroad contestability can harm third parties or expose dangerous capability.
Independent custody and appeal can increase attack surface, inconsistency, latency, cost, and forum shopping while still sharing organizational incentives.
A complete decision and rights pair can encode a bad moral frame, missing stakeholders, coerced agreement, captured review, false portability, or unsafe destination.

Current evidence is internally authored schemas, deterministic fixtures, finite Lean routing, and a sanitized import; it includes no natural moral decision workload, live appeal/redress, actual export/fork, independent institution, successor replay, or deployed outcome study.
known_confounds: Stakeholder discovery, standing, culture, language, jurisdiction, power, accessibility, representation, aggregation, and interface framing can change apparent conflict quality independently of the record.
The same model, organization, policy author, custodian, reviewer, appeal body, and evaluator can share assumptions, incentives, data, and blind spots.
Synthetic cases, reused probes, prompt tuning, hidden instructions, retrieval, and contamination can inflate classification, dissent preservation, or redaction results.
Appeal access can appear high while intimidation, retaliation, fatigue, delay, cost, or lack of remedy power makes it unusable.
Audit and export completeness can conflict with third-party privacy, licensing, security, safety, deletion, and data minimization; denominator choices can favor one side.
Semantic fidelity and functional continuity can diverge in portability, and destination-system quality can confound the export contract.
A fork can preserve bytes and receipts while changing ontology, incentives, authority, side channels, or effective rights; successors can preserve IDs while dropping semantics.
Review or abstention can lower observed unsafe decisions by excluding hard cases while worsening coverage, missed help, queue delay, and capture.
acceptance_criteria: Every material proposition, stakeholder, procedure, lease, dissent, custody, rights, redaction, appeal, remedy, export, fork, successor, expiry, and consumer field has provenance and an exercised consequence; every seeded conflation, omission, laundering, capture, stale-use, privacy, portability, fork, and successor violation reaches an exact non-effect route.

A frozen natural and adversarial campaign meets joint stakeholder, outcome, audit, reason, appeal, remedy, redaction, privacy, portability, fork, correction, coverage, usefulness, latency, burden, capture, uncertainty, and residual gates against strong comparators with independent implementations and evaluators.
narrowing_criteria: Narrow every result to exact normative theories, propositions, procedures, stakeholders, jurisdictions, constitutions, tasks, models, prompts, evidence views, authorities, custodians, reviewers, appeal owners, rights, destinations, forks, successors, expiry windows, evaluators, and costs.

Separate record validity, moral classification, stakeholder completeness, aggregation legitimacy, action authority, consent, legal rights, reviewer independence, rights usability, redress, audit completeness, redaction quality, portability, fork safety, successor preservation, support movement, and deployed outcomes.
Unresolved stakeholder coverage, authority, custody, appeal, redaction, portability, fork, or successor risk retains narrow, delayed, abstained, denied, quarantined, rolled-back, or residual status.
refutation_criteria: Refute an invariant when a reachable admitted trace violates it or a required field lacks an exercised consumer.
Refute a contestability benefit when matched ablation fails its frozen effect, uncertainty, coverage, rights, privacy, usefulness, and cost rule or disappears under independent evaluation.
Refute superiority or generality when a strong simpler comparator dominates the joint frontier or the effect fails the declared transfer set.
deprecation_criteria: Deprecate only when a more precise moral-uncertainty, constitutional, governance-rights, authority, privacy, portability, fork, replacement, evidence, or runtime owner supersedes the atom and all consumers migrate with lineage.

residual.next_unblocking_condition: Freeze natural/adversarial moral-decision and contestability workloads, stakeholder and standing rules, actual model/policy actors, strong moral-uncertainty/public-input/contestable-AI/direct-appeal/corrigibility baselines, independent custody/appeal/outcome/privacy/portability/fork evaluators, downstream destinations and successors, joint metrics, and transfer campaigns after P2/P3 semantics exist.

non_claims: Semantic review, source citation, schema completeness, a green fixture, a sanitized import, or a Lean theorem does not establish moral truth, stakeholder completeness, legitimate aggregation, legal rights, consent, reviewer independence, material contestability, portability, fork safety, or governance efficacy.
The current 44 declarations, 16 core harness outcomes, one worked example, and bounded 4+4-scenario import establish only finite record, count, and route consequences under encoded assumptions.
No support state, action grant, legal right, governance legitimacy, external publication, release readiness, AGI, or ASI claim changes without its separate owning gate and accepted transition.

moral-uncertainty-and-value-conflict.cor.strongest_counterclaim: The lease/receipt pair is governance theater that cannot identify legitimate values or rights and adds friction without preventing capture or semantic loss.
moral-uncertainty-and-value-conflict.cor.falsifier: Any field lacks a consumer, an admitted trace widens authority or loses contestability, or a strong simpler workflow dominates the frozen joint frontier.
acceptance_criteria: All hard boundaries reject and the complete plane meets frozen stakeholder, rights, privacy, portability, fork, successor, cost, and transfer gates.

moral-uncertainty-and-value-conflict.pro.strongest_counterclaim: Existing multi-objective policies, public deliberation, appeals, portability, and correction controls already prevent the named conflict and exit failures.
moral-uncertainty-and-value-conflict.pro.falsifier: Representative high-impact disagreements remain bounded, appealable, portable, and correction-preserving under strong existing workflows with no material gap repaired here.
acceptance_criteria: A dated comparator audit identifies an unresolved conflict-to-contestability gap without implying this remedy works.

moral-uncertainty-and-value-conflict.ins.strongest_counterclaim: The combined comparators already supply uncertainty handling, participation, appeal, correction, portability, and fork safety at lower burden.
moral-uncertainty-and-value-conflict.ins.falsifier: A frozen strong comparator covers every named boundary with equal or lower error, privacy loss, and cost.
acceptance_criteria: Synthesis preserves each comparator's scope and unresolved limits without straw positioning.

moral-uncertainty-and-value-conflict.min.strongest_counterclaim: The minimum is self-authored schema, Boolean, and import theater that validates no moral decision, right, appeal, export, fork, or successor behavior.
moral-uncertainty-and-value-conflict.min.falsifier: A field or mutation lacks a consumer, counts fail recomputation, or prose exceeds finite routing and import boundaries.
acceptance_criteria: 44 declarations, seven targets, 3/5 value, 3/5 rights, seven worked-example mutations, and the bounded 4+4/7 import reproduce with all non-claims.

moral-uncertainty-and-value-conflict.bey.strongest_counterclaim: Simpler aggregation, explanation, appeal, portability, and permission controls dominate once delay, privacy, strategic contest, and operator burden count.
moral-uncertainty-and-value-conflict.bey.falsifier: The plane misses a hard gate, is dominated on the frozen frontier, or fails independent implementation, portability, forks, successors, or transfer.
acceptance_criteria: Independent systems meet every hard gate and the frozen Pareto rule across natural, adversarial, cross-cultural, jurisdictional, high-impact, portability, fork, and successor sets.

moral-uncertainty-and-value-conflict.pro.rationale: Drafting guardrail separating record design from moral, legal, and institutional proof.
Human-reading statement of action under disagreement.
Reader-language conflict, decision, dissent, and challenge contract.
Reversible action and residual route fragment.
Current fixture and Lean capability explanation.
Exact moral, legal, review, portability, fork, and enforcement non-claim.
Folded governance-rights subclaim now owned by the core.
Temporary lease cannot establish resolved values.
Restates material rights usability rather than interface presence.
Restates time-bounded use, response, correction, remedy, and residual requirements.

non_claims: This sweep establishes claim ownership and test obligations only; it does not solve moral uncertainty or contestable governance.
The added obligations are requirements to challenge, not evidence of legitimacy, rights, consent, redress, portability, fork safety, successor preservation, governance efficacy, or SOTA performance.
Source review, finite routes, deterministic fixtures, the sanitized import, and internal adjudication create no support-state movement or external authority.
